conventions_srcapiauth_1651853860540.content: AuthService test pattern: mock @/getPayload, new JwtService('test-secret'), new AuthService(mockPayload, jwtService), vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach — apply to all auth endpoint tests.

thoughts_srcapiauthlogints_1651855456921.content: login.ts was using UserStore SHA-256 while register.ts used AuthService PBKDF2 — these must be unified so Payload is the single auth source.

facts_srcapiauthlogints_1651857181343.content: login.ts becomes a thin wrapper; all user lookup, PBKDF2 verification (25000 iters), token gen, and refresh token storage live in AuthService.login().

facts_srcapiauthregisterts_1651858575205.content: register.ts already delegates to authService.login() at line 69 — login.ts follows the same delegation pattern.

thoughts_srcapiauthregisterts_1888664383.content: An endpoint that appears unchanged may still have been wrong — register.ts already delegated to authService but was overlooked

facts_srcapiauthlogints_1888665823042.content: Consolidation work may reveal the target endpoint was already correct; scope the change to only what is actually broken

facts_srcapiauth_1888667399368.content: Delegation pattern (service.login() call) is preferred over inline auth logic in route handlers

thoughts_auth_1888669211225.content: Dual auth anti-pattern: endpoints using different mechanisms (in-memory SHA-256 vs Payload PBKDF2) must be unified to one source
